title: Concurrency, Cancellation, Timeouts, Retries, and Flake Control · Stakeholder Delivery Mission
summary: A community scheduling API team. The team needs clean Node and TypeScript builds, layered gates and tests, matrices, immutable reports, safe caching, cancellation, timeout, and flake evidence under changed environments. Planned artifact: Deliver Reproducible TypeScript Quality Pipeline with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use npm ci with a committed matching lockfile and committed install flags while detecting lock drift and registry changes.
Design stable job names, required status checks, merge queues, branch rules, bypass authority, and migration without accidental deadlock.
Upload unique immutable artifacts with digest, producer, revision, content scope, retention, hidden-file policy, and no-secret review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-gb6zgy". Tailor its execution-control.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, keep Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-gb6zgy
  - competency: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair
    artifact: execution-control.yaml
    decision: flake detection quarantine repair
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions
    fault-injection: make the service container start before it is ready
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: gb6zgy
    change: |
      concurrency-group: deploy-production-service-a
      cancel-in-progress: tests-only
      revision-guard: compare-before-mutate
      job-timeout-minutes: 20
      command-timeout-seconds: 90
      cleanup: always-idempotent
      retry: classified-transient-max-2
      first-failure: retained
      flake-owner-expiry: team-delivery-7-days
    verify: simulate overlapping revisions cancellation timeout transient failure duplicate side effect and cleanup interruption
    failure: Reject blanket cancellation, commit-order assumptions, timeout as cleanup, all-error retry, or ownerless quarantine.
    repair: Scope concurrency to shared state, reject stale mutation, bound every wait, preserve first failure, and expire quarantine.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
